title: 5 Rules to Write Copy That Sells (infographic)
author: Rory Sutherland (Vice Chairman, Ogilvy UK) / Daniel Bustamante
rules: Rule 1: Write more conversationally than feels comfortable — sound like a bar conversation, not a boardroom
Rule 2: Verbs > Adjectives > Adverbs — strong verbs create mental images instantly, weak words dilute
Rule 3: Anglo-Saxon words over Latin words — short punchy words hit harder, build trust
Rule 4: Convert features into benefits — nobody cares what it does, only what it does for them
Rule 5: Sometimes just tell people a fact — skip the persuasion, a compelling fact does the selling
supporting_quote: The best copywriting doesn't feel like copywriting at all. It feels like a conversation you want to have.
cta_gating_strategy: Comment 'RORY' [keyword] to get the AI prompt that applies these rules — keyword-gated lead gen play
format_steal: Vintage newspaper infographic — serif-heavy, cream background, structured rule + example pairs. High shareability format for LinkedIn.
nebula_adaptation: Patched Nebula_Voice_DNA.md with 4 rule additions: (1) Conversational framing in Sentence Structure ('more conversational than comfortable'), (2) Verb hierarchy in Word Choice (Verbs > Adjectives > Adverbs + adverb cut list), (3) 12-pair Anglo-Saxon vs Latin word mapping in Word Choice, (4) Fact Drop Technique section (Rule 5) with 4 rules + Nebula examples. Added Fact Drop cold email framework (fact_drop) to cold_email_frameworks.json. Added keyword-gated CTA strategy to Voice DNA CTA Format section. Added Infographic job type to content_calendar_meta.json. Updated Enforcement section with Anglo-Saxon word check, adverb scan, and fact drop test.
